title: conduct.v1 — the frontmatter of a codes-of-conduct layer file
description: A code of conduct is one plain-language behavioral stance for how the AI engages with the operator (the tier-3 guidance surface; pure posture, never an enforcement gate). The two committed layers — .engine/conduct/defaults.md (the engine's universal stance, engine-owned) and .engine/conduct/operator.md (this deployment's own stance, operator config) — are each a single Markdown file whose frontmatter carries the `codes` list (each code's stable id, short title, and lifecycle status) and, on the operator layer only, an optional `disables` list naming engine defaults to drop. The prose body holds one '## <title>' section per code (the rule itself, optionally a rationale); the body-to-frontmatter correspondence is checked separately by the conduct-shape rule. This schema governs only the frontmatter and is resolved through the surface catalog (the 'conduct' surface) by the conduct-frontmatter check at the merge. An empty codes list is valid — the public template ships an empty operator override that each deployment fills.

codes.description: The codes of conduct this layer declares, in the order they are presented. May be empty (an as-yet-unfilled operator override). Each entry pairs with a matching '## <title>' section in the body.

id.description: The code's stable identifier: 'conduct-' followed by a lower-case, hyphenated slug (for example 'conduct-plain-language'). It stays fixed so composition by id (operator supersedes a matching default; a disables entry drops one) is deterministic.

title.description: A short, plain-language label for the stance (for example 'Speak in plain language'). One line, no trailing punctuation. It must match the text of this code's '## <title>' section heading in the body.

status.description: Where this code sits in the artifact lifecycle. active = in force; deprecated = on the way out but still read; retired = no longer in force, kept for history.

disables.description: OPERATOR LAYER ONLY. A list of engine default code ids to drop from the active stance (for example a default that does not fit this project). Present only in operator.md; on the defaults layer it is flagged by the conduct-shape check.
